title: The Street Where Gentleness Learned to Spread

features.name: house-keeper's record
features.desc-on-touch: each house holds a door, a window, a place where someone's change began and stayed kind. the archive reads these alterations as if they were written down.

features.name: spreading gentleness
features.desc-on-touch: the change crossed from one family to the next not as infection but as habit learned and passed forward. kindness as contagion.

features.name: alterations kept secret
features.desc-on-touch: each family keeping a different secret — what they changed into, how permanent it was, whether they chose it or it chose them.

features.name: thorough kindness marker
features.desc-on-touch: a standing place that marks: the change was not violent; it was thorough; it was permanent; it was also kind. all four things at once.

features.name: gentle settling
features.desc-on-touch: the streets themselves learned the feel of this spreading change. dust settles differently here, as if even the air has accepted what happened.

inscription: the neighbourhood learned that change could be permanent and still feel like coming home.

file.name: Altered_Street_Families_Records_1982-1991.txt
file.note: documented: each house tells a different version of the same kindness

reverie.title: REVERIE — the street where nothing stayed the same but every
narr: You walk with her down a street where families
are becoming something else — not fast, not violent.
In one yard, children play games with new rules
they seem to know by heart already. A woman watches
from a porch, and Katita squeezes your hand. 'They're
choosing this,' she says. 'They're choosing to stay.'

exits.label: back eastward to the house of changed children
westward into deeper alteration, where change learned to speak
northward to where the kindness reaches its edge
southward where other families keep their versions
